message:
chore(fcp-e2e): fan out CapabilityVerifier::verify() owned-token signature to 4 more harnesses + routine beads sync

Continues the `fcp-e2e` migration to the new
`CapabilityVerifier::verify(token: T, ...) where T:
Into<CapabilityToken>` signature. Earlier commits covered:

- Batch 1 (10 harnesses): mailchimp, onepassword, retool, segment,
  sendgrid, snowflake, todoist, trello, webhook_receiver, zapier.
- Batch 2 (3 harnesses): algolia, arxiv, asana.

This commit catches up the four remaining harnesses whose dirty-tree
state exposed the old `&req.capability_token` call shape:

- bitbucket: two sites (invoke + plan), both on the standard two-arg
  form `verifier.verify(&req.capability_token, &required_capability,
  &req.operation, &[]).await?`.
- box: same two-site pattern.
- docusign: one site, inside an `if let Some(verifier) = ...` guard
  with a different surrounding shape. Updated to
  `verifier.verify(req.capability_token.clone(), &cap_id,
  &req.operation, &[])?`. A pure `cargo fmt` reflow on the
  `ciborium::into_writer(&constraints, &mut constraints_cbor)` call
  in `build_token` came along for the ride (previously wrapped onto
  two lines, now one) — no behavioural change.
- llm_router: two sites, each behind a verifier-not-configured
  NotConfigured check. Same `.clone()` substitution, plus a
  matching rustfmt reflow on the same `build_token` call shape as
  docusign.

All four harnesses continue to build against the owned-token
verifier signature; no behavioural change beyond matching the
current signature. Once every caller has been flipped, the
deprecated `&CapabilityToken` impl can be removed without breaking
the e2e suite.

Also picks up a single routine beads metadata sync on
`.beads/issues.jsonl` (1 line modified, 0 insertions, 0 deletions —
a status/timestamp field on an existing issue, net zero line-count
change).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2zy9","title":"[IDEA-01] SDK Connector Runtime Kit (Supervised Streaming/Polling)","description":"# SDK Connector Runtime Kit (Supervised Streaming/Polling)

## Goal
Provide a reusable, SDK-level runtime kit for connector authors that encapsulates the reliability patterns from the Discord/Telegram studies: supervised loops, backoff, resume, heartbeat/zombie detection, deterministic shutdown, and structured health transitions.

## Background / Why
Discord (streaming WebSocket) and Telegram (polling) independently implement the same reliability machinery. Without a shared SDK surface we get duplicated bugs, inconsistent health semantics, and regressions across connectors. A runtime kit makes these patterns mechanical and accelerates new connector development.

## Scope
- SDK module(s) used by connector implementations without host changes.
- No changes to external APIs; this is internal SDK ergonomics and correctness.
- Includes testing utilities and reference examples.

## Deliverables
- `fcp_sdk::runtime::{StreamingSupervisor, PollingSupervisor, SupervisorConfig}` with:
  - exponential backoff + jitter
  - retry budgets + cooldowns
  - shutdown signals
  - session resume hooks
  - deterministic health transitions (Healthy/Degraded/Unhealthy)
- Connector-facing trait adapters for platform-specific state (session IDs, cursor offsets).
- Structured logging fields (`connector_id`, `zone_id`, `operation_id`, `stream_key`, `sequence`).

## Tests
- Unit tests for backoff, retry budgets, and shutdown transitions.
- Integration tests with a fake stream/poll source (no external calls).
- Deterministic logging snapshots (JSONL schema once flywheel_connectors-11g2 lands).

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least one streaming connector can replace its custom loop with the SDK supervisor.
- At least one polling connector can replace its custom loop with the SDK supervisor.
- Health transitions are deterministic and observable via logs.","notes":"All child tasks complete/blocked (flywheel_connectors-fgot, flywheel_connectors-uhmy, flywheel_connectors-3zhv closed; flywheel_connectors-19rr completed and blocked). Waiting on dependency flywheel_connectors-1n78.20 to close.

[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.

All types with extensive test coverage (450+ tests in module). Closing.","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:17:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-308","title":"fcp.sentry: Performance monitoring data access","description":"# fcp.sentry: Performance monitoring data access (FCP2)

## Goal
Expose Sentry performance/trace surfaces so agents can:
- identify slow transactions and regressions
- correlate errors with performance degradation
- fetch trace/transaction summaries for RCA

## Scope
### Read operations
- Query transactions by project/environment/time range.
- Fetch a transaction/trace summary:
  - duration
  - spans breakdown
  - key tags
  - sample trace ids
- Fetch performance metrics trends (where available).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `sentry.performance.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Performance data may include URLs, user agents, and other sensitive metadata:
  - treat as tainted
  - avoid logging raw payloads

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `sentry.performance.transactions.query`
  - `sentry.performance.transaction.get`
  - `sentry.performance.trace.get_summary`
- Pagination helpers + time-window query helpers.

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - query construction and pagination
  - normalization of key metrics
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff
  - taint propagation

## Acceptance criteria
- Agents can retrieve stable, comparable performance summaries for RCA.
- No secrets/PII leak through logs.

All clippy clean, 55 tests pass, golden vectors verified.","created_at":"2026-03-09T18:48:34Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-3091d","title":"[AUDIT][HIGH] fcp-mesh degraded control-plane accepts tampered symbol payloads without AEAD verification","description":"P1 targeted audit. Evidence: crates/fcp-mesh/src/degraded.rs:208-218 encodes CONTROL_PLANE SymbolRecord entries with plaintext data and placeholder zero auth_tag values; crates/fcp-mesh/src/degraded.rs:580-587 then feeds symbol.data directly into RaptorQDecoder with only a comment saying auth_tag verification would happen in production; crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:1843-1849 routes raw FcpsFrame input into degraded_decoder.process_frame without any per-symbol integrity check at the MeshNode boundary. Repro: encode a ControlPlaneEnvelope with DegradedModeEncoder::encode, flip one byte in frame.symbols[0].data (leave auth_tag unchanged), then call MeshNode::decode_control_plane or DegradedModeDecoder::process_frame; the tampered symbol is still admitted to reconstruction instead of being rejected as forged. Impact: any caller path that reaches degraded CONTROL_PLANE decoding before a lower transport layer has authenticated symbol contents can inject or corrupt control-plane payloads despite ENCRYPTED/CONTROL_PLANE flags. Suggested fix: wire zone-key-based SymbolEnvelope decrypt/auth_tag verification into degraded decode before decoder.add_symbol, and fail closed until authenticated symbol decryption is available rather than accepting placeholder tags.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T16:31:44.621497Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T16:42:59.894018Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-3091d","title":"[AUDIT][HIGH] fcp-mesh degraded control-plane accepts tampered symbol payloads without AEAD verification","description":"P1 targeted audit. Evidence: crates/fcp-mesh/src/degraded.rs:208-218 encodes CONTROL_PLANE SymbolRecord entries with plaintext data and placeholder zero auth_tag values; crates/fcp-mesh/src/degraded.rs:580-587 then feeds symbol.data directly into RaptorQDecoder with only a comment saying auth_tag verification would happen in production; crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:1843-1849 routes raw FcpsFrame input into degraded_decoder.process_frame without any per-symbol integrity check at the MeshNode boundary. Repro: encode a ControlPlaneEnvelope with DegradedModeEncoder::encode, flip one byte in frame.symbols[0].data (leave auth_tag unchanged), then call MeshNode::decode_control_plane or DegradedModeDecoder::process_frame; the tampered symbol is still admitted to reconstruction instead of being rejected as forged. Impact: any caller path that reaches degraded CONTROL_PLANE decoding before a lower transport layer has authenticated symbol contents can inject or corrupt control-plane payloads despite ENCRYPTED/CONTROL_PLANE flags. Suggested fix: wire zone-key-based SymbolEnvelope decrypt/auth_tag verification into degraded decode before decoder.add_symbol, and fail closed until authenticated symbol decryption is available rather than accepting placeholder tags.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T16:31:44.621497Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T17:01:24.313458Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T17:01:24.313123Z","close_reason":"Verified degraded control-plane symbols with zone-key AEAD before decode and added tamper rejection regression","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-30bf","title":"[IDEA-07] Safe Formatting Helpers (HTML/Markdown → Plaintext Fallback)","description":"# Safe Formatting Helpers (HTML/Markdown → Plaintext Fallback)

## Goal
Provide SDK utilities for safe message formatting that automatically fall back to plaintext when the target platform rejects markup.

## Background / Why
Telegram in particular rejects malformed HTML entities; a robust fallback prevents message loss. This pattern is reusable for other platforms with strict markup parsers.

## Scope
- SDK helper that attempts formatting and falls back when parse errors are detected.
- Connector-facing API that can plug into send_message operations.

## Deliverables
- `Formatter` helper with `render_with_fallback()`.
- Error classifier for parse/format errors.
- Example usage in Telegram-style connector.

## Tests
- Unit tests for fallback behavior.
- Integration test that simulates parse failure and verifies fallback send.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Formatting failures never drop messages; they fallback to plaintext.
- Errors are logged without leaking message content.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add metamorphic tests (encode->decode->encode) plus differential oracle for edge cases.
- Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Parallelize vector generation with deterministic seeds.
- Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost.
